summary: Scorching July 4th with blazing Scarlet>Fire and Brent's key brilliance
blurb: The show shines with a fiery Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain stretch and a soaring Just A Little Light. Brent Mydland’s keyboards dominate while Jerry delivers energetic, short‑clad guitar work throughout. The second set flows from Victim Or The Crime into Foolish Heart and closes strong with U.S. Blues.
review: July 4, 1990 at the Sandstone Amphitheatre captured a summer‑kickoff Dead show that still feels hot in the ears. From the opening “Cold Rain And Snow” through a tight first set, the band built momentum that exploded in the second set: Victim Or The Crime segued into Foolish Heart, then lifted into a blistering Just A Little Light before launching the legendary Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain sequence. Brent Mydland’s keyboard work was nothing short of beast‑mode, soaring over the jam and driving the band forward. Jerry Garcia, in his signature shorts, delivered an energetic guitar tone that fans repeatedly praised, while Phil Lesh laid down a solid bass foundation. The drummers kept the groove tight, with Mickey Hart’s “i‑beam” rumble adding extra power. Highlights continued with Space → The Wheel and a heartfelt Stella Blue before the band wrapped up with Sugar Magnolia and a rousing U.S. Blues encore. Across all available sources the performance is consistently described as “on fire,” making this one of the standout summer shows of ’90.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Eight of nine recordings describe the show positively, repeatedly praising the Scarlet>Fire jam, Brent's keyboards on Just A Little Light, and overall band energy. The lone mixed review still notes strong moments, so consensus is strongly favorable, warranting a high positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard source with clear overall mix, captures the full set, and is highlighted by reviewers as the most complete and representative version despite minor bass thinness.

key_highlights: Blazing Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain sequence
Brent Mydland’s soaring Just A Little Light
Victim Or The Crime → Foolish Heart transition
Space → The Wheel segue and a heartfelt Stella Blue

band_performance.Jerry: Energetic, short‑clad guitar work that fans repeatedly called ‘on fire’; his tone was praised across recordings, with only minor notes of being buried in the mix on one tape.
band_performance.Phil: Solid bass foundation; pre‑drums segment highlighted as excellent and overall steady support throughout the set.
band_performance.Bob: Delivered a key vocal moment on Victim Or The Crime; while some reviews note limited vocal presence elsewhere, his contribution to that song is consistently praised.
band_performance.Brent: A beast on keyboards – soaring lines on Just A Little Light and driving textures on Scarlet Begonias/Fire; many reviewers cite this as one of his final great performances.
band_performance.Mickey: Powerful “i‑beam” rumble noted in audience recordings, adding extra punch to the rhythm section.
band_performance.Billy: Standard but well‑played drumming throughout; no specific critiques, indicating reliable support for the band's improvisations.
